Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated creatinine, proteinuria, and abnormal urine findings (cloudy, brown, low specific gravity, bilirubinuria, glucosuria, ketonuria), suggesting significant renal impairment with possible nephrotic features. Low HbA1c and normal serum glucose argue against chronic hyperglycemia. Mild metabolic acidosis (low CO₂), hyperchloremia, and abnormal urine color/odor may indicate underlying renal or hepatic dysfunction. Recommend urgent nephrology evaluation and further workup for acute or chronic kidney disease.
